target.title: fix: preflight malformed OpenShell exec commands
target.body:
﻿Fixes #72373.

## Summary
- Add a shared SSH/OpenShell exec command preflight before remote `/bin/sh -c` execution.
- Reject unresolved generated placeholders like `<workflow-id>` and common malformed shell input such as unclosed quotes, trailing escapes, unterminated command substitutions, and unterminated here-docs.
- Preserve legitimate shell syntax including redirection, compact redirection, here-doc bodies, process substitution, arithmetic expansion, quoted placeholder-looking text, and comments.
- Build validated remote exec commands before opening SSH/OpenShell sessions so malformed input fails before session setup.
- Preserve the existing public `buildExecRemoteCommand` quote-only contract, and route SSH/OpenShell runtime paths through the explicitly named `buildValidatedExecRemoteCommand` helper.

This follows ClawSweeper's suggested fix shape: keep the existing loop-detection behavior and add the remaining focused validator at the shared SSH/OpenShell command boundary.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: malformed generated exec commands such as `workflow run <workflow-id> --ref main` now fail before SSH/OpenShell session or runtime setup.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw PR head `07bfc06675` on my Windows desktop, Docker Desktop `29.4.1`, a disposable Debian `bookworm-slim` container running a real `sshd`, Windows OpenSSH client, and OpenClaw's real `createSshSandboxBackend` / `buildExecSpec` path. This exercises the shared SSH exec boundary used by OpenShell; no VPS or live OpenShell account was used for this proof.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started a throwaway Docker SSH server, created a real SSH sandbox backend pointed at it, ran one valid backend exec to prove the remote path worked, then created a fresh backend and attempted `workflow run <workflow-id> --ref main`.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```text
REAL_SSH_VALID_EXIT=0
REAL_SSH_VALID_STDOUT="/tmp/openclaw-72373-proof/openclaw-ssh-pr85874-proof-valid-d6495223/workspace\nseed-from-host"
MALFORMED_COMMAND_ERROR="Malformed SSH/OpenShell exec command: unresolved placeholder token <workflow-id>."
BAD_RUNTIME_ID=openclaw-ssh-pr85874-proof-bad-1e0d9052
BAD_RUNTIME_CHECK="RUNTIME_NOT_CREATED"
REMOTE_CLEANUP="REMOTE_CLEANED"
LOCAL_PROOF_CLEANUP=done
REMOVED_CONTAINER=oc72373-proof-1779595741
```

- Observed result after fix: the valid real SSH backend command executed and read the synced workspace seed file. The malformed generated command failed with the new preflight error, and the fresh bad-command runtime directory was not created on the remote SSH host.
- What was not tested: a live NVIDIA OpenShell CLI/account/gateway run. The proof uses the real SSH backend because the fixed validator and fail-before-session behavior are in the shared SSH/OpenShell exec boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current main passed raw `params.command` into the shared `/bin/sh -c` command builder without this preflight, and OpenShell built the command only after sandbox/session setup.

## Proof
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts -t "keeps the public exec command builder quote-only|rejects malformed generated exec commands|allows shell features and quoted placeholder-looking text|uses the shared SSH exec command preflight|rejects malformed exec commands before opening" --reporter verbose` -> passed, 3 files / 26 tests passed, 30 skipped
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/sandbox/ssh.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh-backend.ts src/agents/sandbox.ts src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.ts extensions/openshell/src/backend.ts extensions/openshell/src/cli.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts` -> passed
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` -> passed
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed
- `pnpm tsgo:test` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

## Root Cause
- Root cause: generated placeholder-like shell text could reach the SSH/OpenShell runtime command boundary without validation.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the runtime path only quoted the final shell argv; it did not reject malformed generated commands before runtime/session setup.
- Contributing context: the issue's loop-abort half is already handled on main, so this PR intentionally keeps the fix focused on the remaining SSH/OpenShell command-preflight gap.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this: unit tests plus backend-boundary regression tests.
- Target test or file: `src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts` and `extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: malformed placeholder commands, unclosed quotes, trailing escapes, and unterminated shell constructs fail before SSH/OpenShell session setup, while valid shell syntax remains allowed.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives at the shared command construction boundary used by both SSH and OpenShell exec paths. The PR also locks the public `buildExecRemoteCommand` helper as quote-only for compatibility.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Malformed generated SSH/OpenShell exec commands now fail fast with a clear `Malformed SSH/OpenShell exec command` error instead of being handed to the remote shell. The existing public `buildExecRemoteCommand` helper remains quote-only for plugin compatibility.

## Summary

- Problem: When `hooks.token` equals `gateway.auth.password`, any caller that only knows the hook ingress bearer can authenticate directly to password-mode Gateway HTTP surfaces. That bypasses hook-specific routing controls and upgrades a hook token into full operator access, including owner-only tool behavior on `/tools/invoke`.

- What changed: Add the missing security-audit coverage for password-mode reuse.

target.title: fix(agents): remap /btw transcript after compaction
target.body:
## Summary
- remap `/btw` session transcript resolution from a matched pre-compaction checkpoint transcript back to the active post-compaction transcript
- keep Codex side-question binding lookup pointed at the active transcript sidecar instead of a checkpoint path with no binding
- add focused regression coverage for both the resolver and the Codex harness `/btw` path

Closes #86393.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex `/btw` side questions should resolve back to the active post-compaction transcript when the stored session entry still points at a pre-compaction checkpoint transcript.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout running the production `resolveBtwSessionTranscriptPath()` implementation through `node --import tsx`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --eval "import { resolveBtwSessionTranscriptPath } from './src/agents/btw-transcript.ts'; const storePath='/tmp/openclaw-btw-session/sessions.json'; const checkpointFile='/tmp/openclaw-btw-session/session-1.checkpoint.98904e8a-a402-4d92-8795-4a2d7bba2276.jsonl'; const postCompactionFile='/tmp/openclaw-btw-session/session-1.jsonl'; const sessionEntry={ sessionId:'session-1', sessionFile:checkpointFile, updatedAt:1, compactionCheckpoints:[{ checkpointId:'98904e8a-a402-4d92-8795-4a2d7bba2276', sessionKey:'agent:main:telegram:direct:user', sessionId:'session-1', createdAt:1, reason:'manual', preCompaction:{ sessionId:'session-1', sessionFile:checkpointFile, leafId:'pre-leaf' }, postCompaction:{ sessionId:'session-1', sessionFile:postCompactionFile, leafId:'post-leaf' } }] }; console.log(resolveBtwSessionTranscriptPath({ sessionId:'session-1', sessionEntry, sessionKey:'agent:main:telegram:direct:user', storePath }));"`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): terminal output from the command above:
```text
$ node --import tsx --eval "import { resolveBtwSessionTranscriptPath } from './src/agents/btw-transcript.ts'; ..."
/tmp/openclaw-btw-session/session-1.jsonl
```
- Observed result after fix: with a stored checkpoint transcript as input, the production resolver returns the active `session-1.jsonl` transcript path instead of keeping `/btw` on the `.checkpoint...jsonl` path.
- What was not tested: no live end-to-end Codex app-server `/btw` run against a real persisted `.codex-app-server.json` sidecar.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proof exercises the real runtime resolver directly in a local checkout, but it does not include a GUI or networked Codex session.

## Tests and validation
Which commands did you run?
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- added a Codex `/btw` compaction regression to `src/agents/btw.test.ts`

What failed before this fix, if known?
- `/btw` could keep the checkpoint transcript path, so Codex side-question binding lookup missed the active transcript sidecar and treated the thread as inactive

If no test was added, why not?
- tests were added

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
- Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
- No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

How is that risk mitigated?

target.title: fix(pi-embedded): route openai+codex auth through openai-codex on compaction fallback (#86373)
target.body:
Implements the fix proposed inline in #86373.

## Problem

The normal embedded Pi run path already normalizes `provider=openai` + `authProfileId=openai-codex:*` to the `openai-codex` runtime provider via `resolveSelectedOpenAIPiRuntimeProvider`. But `resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget` returned the caller provider directly when no `compaction.model` override was configured, leaving the compaction/context-engine fallback in an inconsistent state:

- `provider = openai`
- `authProfileId = openai-codex:…`
- `model = gpt-*`

That state enters the plain OpenAI API-key path and fails with `No API key found for provider openai`, even though the active session is using Codex OAuth. Most visible after a stale Codex app-server thread binding, because the fallback is then used for compaction.

## Fix

Apply the same `resolveSelectedOpenAIPiRuntimeProvider` routing in the compaction target resolver so the main and compaction paths agree on the runtime provider. The override-present branch is unchanged.

Added regression tests:
- `openai + openai-codex:default` resolves to `openai-codex` and preserves the auth profile
- non-openai providers (`anthropic`) are unchanged

Closes #86373

target.title: fix: preflight malformed OpenShell exec commands
target.body:
﻿Fixes #72373.

## Summary
- Add a shared SSH/OpenShell exec command preflight before remote `/bin/sh -c` execution.
- Reject unresolved generated placeholders like `<workflow-id>` and common malformed shell input such as unclosed quotes, trailing escapes, unterminated command substitutions, and unterminated here-docs.
- Preserve legitimate shell syntax including redirection, compact redirection, here-doc bodies, process substitution, arithmetic expansion, quoted placeholder-looking text, and comments.
- Build validated remote exec commands before opening SSH/OpenShell sessions so malformed input fails before session setup.
- Preserve the existing public `buildExecRemoteCommand` quote-only contract, and route SSH/OpenShell runtime paths through the explicitly named `buildValidatedExecRemoteCommand` helper.

This follows ClawSweeper's suggested fix shape: keep the existing loop-detection behavior and add the remaining focused validator at the shared SSH/OpenShell command boundary.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: malformed generated exec commands such as `workflow run <workflow-id> --ref main` now fail before SSH/OpenShell session or runtime setup.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw PR head `07bfc06675` on my Windows desktop, Docker Desktop `29.4.1`, a disposable Debian `bookworm-slim` container running a real `sshd`, Windows OpenSSH client, and OpenClaw's real `createSshSandboxBackend` / `buildExecSpec` path. This exercises the shared SSH exec boundary used by OpenShell; no VPS or live OpenShell account was used for this proof.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started a throwaway Docker SSH server, created a real SSH sandbox backend pointed at it, ran one valid backend exec to prove the remote path worked, then created a fresh backend and attempted `workflow run <workflow-id> --ref main`.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```text
REAL_SSH_VALID_EXIT=0
REAL_SSH_VALID_STDOUT="/tmp/openclaw-72373-proof/openclaw-ssh-pr85874-proof-valid-d6495223/workspace\nseed-from-host"
MALFORMED_COMMAND_ERROR="Malformed SSH/OpenShell exec command: unresolved placeholder token <workflow-id>."
BAD_RUNTIME_ID=openclaw-ssh-pr85874-proof-bad-1e0d9052
BAD_RUNTIME_CHECK="RUNTIME_NOT_CREATED"
REMOTE_CLEANUP="REMOTE_CLEANED"
LOCAL_PROOF_CLEANUP=done
REMOVED_CONTAINER=oc72373-proof-1779595741
```

- Observed result after fix: the valid real SSH backend command executed and read the synced workspace seed file. The malformed generated command failed with the new preflight error, and the fresh bad-command runtime directory was not created on the remote SSH host.
- What was not tested: a live NVIDIA OpenShell CLI/account/gateway run. The proof uses the real SSH backend because the fixed validator and fail-before-session behavior are in the shared SSH/OpenShell exec boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current main passed raw `params.command` into the shared `/bin/sh -c` command builder without this preflight, and OpenShell built the command only after sandbox/session setup.

## Proof
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts -t "keeps the public exec command builder quote-only|rejects malformed generated exec commands|allows shell features and quoted placeholder-looking text|uses the shared SSH exec command preflight|rejects malformed exec commands before opening" --reporter verbose` -> passed, 3 files / 26 tests passed, 30 skipped
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/sandbox/ssh.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh-backend.ts src/agents/sandbox.ts src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.ts extensions/openshell/src/backend.ts extensions/openshell/src/cli.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts` -> passed
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` -> passed
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed
- `pnpm tsgo:test` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

## Root Cause
- Root cause: generated placeholder-like shell text could reach the SSH/OpenShell runtime command boundary without validation.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the runtime path only quoted the final shell argv; it did not reject malformed generated commands before runtime/session setup.
- Contributing context: the issue's loop-abort half is already handled on main, so this PR intentionally keeps the fix focused on the remaining SSH/OpenShell command-preflight gap.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this: unit tests plus backend-boundary regression tests.
- Target test or file: `src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts` and `extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: malformed placeholder commands, unclosed quotes, trailing escapes, and unterminated shell constructs fail before SSH/OpenShell session setup, while valid shell syntax remains allowed.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives at the shared command construction boundary used by both SSH and OpenShell exec paths. The PR also locks the public `buildExecRemoteCommand` helper as quote-only for compatibility.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Malformed generated SSH/OpenShell exec commands now fail fast with a clear `Malformed SSH/OpenShell exec command` error instead of being handed to the remote shell. The existing public `buildExecRemoteCommand` helper remains quote-only for plugin compatibility.

target.title: fix(elevenlabs): accept `voice` as alias for `voiceId` (#86180)
target.body:
## Problem

`talk.providers.elevenlabs.voice` is accepted silently by the gateway config loader (no schema error) but is **never read** by the ElevenLabs speech provider. The provider only looks at `voiceId`, so users who follow the more natural `voice: <id>` shape (which also matches the existing `talk.realtime.voice` field name) silently fall back to `DEFAULT_ELEVENLABS_VOICE_ID` with no warning.

The reporter set `talk.providers.elevenlabs.voice = \"N2lVS1w4EtoT3dr4eOWO\"` (Callum) and got the default voice instead.

## Fix

Accept `voice` as an alias for `voiceId` in:

- `normalizeElevenLabsProviderConfig` (reads from raw `config.providers.elevenlabs`)
- `readElevenLabsProviderConfig` (reads from the already-resolved `SpeechProviderConfig`)

`voiceId` still wins when both are set, so existing configs are unaffected. This brings the per-provider config in line with the realtime config (`talk.realtime.voice`), which is what people expect.

## Test plan

The change is mechanical (one extra nullish fallback per call site). Existing unit tests for `normalizeElevenLabsProviderConfig` continue to pass because `voice` is only consulted when `voiceId` is absent.

Closes #86180

### Description
我为 OpenClaw 框架开发了一个专门用于波场（TRON）网络费用优化的技能插件：`tron-energy`。

### Why merge this?
* **痛点解决**：很多运行机器人和收单系统的用户面临 TRON 网络转账（特别是 USDT）手续费高昂的问题。本插件支持全自动秒级调用高可用能量池，能帮框架使用者直接降低 70% - 80% 的交易手续费。
* **规范安全**：项目已经过严格测试，规范配置了 `.gitignore` 安全规则，并完整支持 Python 和 .NET 双环境，代码注释清晰。

希望能将此技能合入官方推荐的社区生态列表中，方便更多开发者使用，非常感谢！

target.title: fix(doctor): warn and continue when cron job store is unreadable
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw doctor` aborts mid-run when the legacy cron job store exists but is unreadable by the runtime user (the Docker repro in #86102 leaves `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json` as `root:root 0600`). `loadCronStore` only treats `ENOENT` as recoverable; every other read or parse failure rethrows, the legacy cron contribution awaits without a per-contribution catch (`src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`), and later health checks never run.

This PR keeps runtime cron loading strict and only softens the doctor contribution: it catches the load failure inside `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore`, emits a `Cron` warning naming the store path with the underlying reason, and returns so subsequent health checks continue.

Closes #86102.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Test

## Scope

- [x] CLI / Doctor

## Linked Issue

- Closes #86102

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: When `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json` exists but is unreadable to the OpenClaw process (Docker root-owned 0600 file in #86102), `loadCronStore` throws a non-`ENOENT` error that propagates through `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` and the doctor contribution loop, so `openclaw doctor` cannot complete the rest of the health checks.

**Real environment tested**: macOS Darwin 25.4.0 arm64, Node v22.22.0, openclaw branch `fix/doctor-cron-unreadable-store` rebased on upstream main commit `538fd32954267635cf2fef90c0f3c3ce77a91ece`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Created a temp cron store file and removed all permissions (`chmod 0000`) to reproduce the EACCES read failure from #86102 without Docker.
2. Ran the patched `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` via `node --import tsx` pointing at the unreadable store to simulate the doctor cron contribution.
3. Confirmed the function returns cleanly, emits a `Cron` warning with the store path and EACCES reason, and prints "later health checks will continue".
4. Ran focused regression test: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts` (16/16 pass).
5. Drift proof: reverted src patch, re-ran tests — new test fails on EISDIR rethrow; restored — 16/16 pass.
6. Format, lint, typecheck all clean.

**Evidence after fix**:

Real CLI reproduction against an unreadable cron store (EACCES):

```
$ node --import tsx -e "
import { maybeRepairLegacyCronStore } from './src/commands/doctor-cron.ts';
await maybeRepairLegacyCronStore({
  cfg: { cron: { store: '/tmp/doctor-cron-86102/cron/jobs.json' } },
  options: {},
  prompter: { confirm: async () => true },
});
"

 Cron ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                                              │
│  Unable to read cron job store at                                            │
│  /tmp/doctor-cron-86102/cron/jobs.json.                                      │
│  - EACCES: permission denied, open                                           │
│    '/tmp/doctor-cron-86102/cron/jobs.json'                                   │
│  Fix the file's permissions or contents and re-run openclaw doctor;          │
│  later health checks will continue.                                          │
│                                                                              │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

=== After fix: doctor continues to next health check ===
```

Focused regression test (16/16 pass):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts

 ✓  commands  ../../src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts (16 tests) 59ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
```

Drift proof (patch reverted — new test must fail):

```
$ git stash push -- src/commands/doctor-cron.ts

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts

 ×  warns and continues when the cron job store cannot be read
   Caused by: Error: EISDIR: illegal operation on a directory, read
       at loadCronStore ../../src/cron/store.ts:218:17
       at Module.maybeRepairLegacyCronStore ../../src/commands/doctor-cron.ts:338:17

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 15 passed (16)

$ git stash pop
```

Format, lint, typecheck:

```
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-cron.ts src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
All matched files use the correct format.

$ pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-cron.ts src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json
(no diagnostics, exit 0)
```

**Observed result after fix**: `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` returns cleanly after emitting a single `Cron` warning ("Unable to read cron job store at ... EACCES: permission denied ... later health checks will continue"). The prompter is never asked. The doctor contribution loop continues to subsequent contributions. On current main the same call throws and aborts the doctor run.

**What was not tested**: A live Docker reproduction with root-owned 0600 inside a container was not executed; the real CLI proof uses a local `chmod 0000` file on macOS which triggers the same `EACCES` path that the reporter observed inside Docker.

## Root Cause

`loadCronStore` (`src/cron/store.ts`) only catches `ENOENT`; any other read or parse failure rethrows. `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` (`src/commands/doctor-cron.ts:338`) awaits it directly, and the legacy cron contribution in the doctor flow (`src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts:413`) awaits each contribution without a per-contribution catch, so an unreadable cron store aborts the whole `openclaw doctor` run.

## Regression Test Plan

Added `warns and continues when the cron job store cannot be read` to `src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts`. The test substitutes a directory at the cron store path (a non-`ENOENT` read failure that mirrors the reporter's permission case without depending on the test runner's effective uid) and asserts (1) `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` resolves, (2) the prompter is never invoked, and (3) a `Cron`-titled note containing both the store path and "later health checks will continue" is emitted. With the patch reverted the new test fails on the rethrown `EISDIR`, confirming the test guards the regression.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `openclaw doctor` no longer aborts when the cron job store is unreadable; it now emits a single `Cron` warning naming the store path plus the underlying error reason and points the user at `openclaw doctor` to retry, then continues with the remaining health checks.
- No change to scheduler runtime behavior: `loadCronStore` still rethrows non-`ENOENT` errors so cron execution fails closed.

## Scope boundary

- Only `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` in `src/commands/doctor-cron.ts` is softened; `loadCronStore` and `loadCronStoreSync` keep their existing throw-on-read-failure contract for the scheduler.
- No changes to the cron file format, doctor prompter contract, or other doctor health contributions.

## Prior art

No prior PR found targeting #86102 at the time of this submission. The closest adjacent work is the existing `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` contribution introduced via the recent doctor cron migrations on `src/commands/doctor-cron.ts`; this PR only adds a defensive catch around `loadCronStore` and an accompanying focused test.

target.title: fix(codex): cap managed app-server trace logs
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes/ref: #86214

Adds startup retention for OpenClaw-managed Codex app-server trace logs under `CODEX_HOME`:

- applies before shared and isolated Codex app-server startup
- rotates `logs_2.sqlite`, `logs_2.sqlite-wal`, and `logs_2.sqlite-shm` when their combined size exceeds the cap
- defaults to a 512 MiB cap and keeps the 2 newest retired snapshots
- exposes and documents env controls for disable/max-bytes/snapshot count
- logs a warning when rotation happens, but does not fail app-server startup if retention itself fails

This deliberately bounds OpenClaw-managed storage without needing to know the native Codex SQLite schema.

## Why

In one affected OpenClaw install, the managed Codex DB grew to ~43 GiB / 14.5M rows in under 8 days, while standalone Codex on the same machine had only an ~81 MiB `~/.codex/logs_2.sqlite`.

The live process chain was:

`openclaw gateway -> codex.js app-server -> native codex app-server --listen stdio://`

The growing file was:

`~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/logs_2.sqlite`

Additional evidence was posted on #86214:

https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86214#issuecomment-4530473714

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw-managed Codex app-server log storage must not grow without a bounded retention path when `logs_2.sqlite` accumulates high-volume response/WebSocket/SSE trace rows.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw checkout on commit `d239d37f18`, using the PR branch's actual `createIsolatedCodexAppServerClient` startup path with a realistic managed per-agent `CODEX_HOME` containing `logs_2.sqlite` and `logs_2.sqlite-wal`. The app-server command was a real stdio subprocess launched by Node, not a mocked `CodexAppServerClient.start`. Supporting production context comes from the affected Mac mini OpenClaw install that produced the ~43 GiB managed `~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/logs_2.sqlite`.
- **Exact steps or command run after fix:** Created a realistic managed `CODEX_HOME`, wrote oversized `logs_2.sqlite` / WAL files, set `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=4`, then launched a fake stdio app-server subprocess through the branch's `createIsolatedCodexAppServerClient`. The fake app-server wrote its startup observation before responding to `initialize`. The committed equivalent verification command is:

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts --testNamePattern "rotates oversized CODEX_HOME logs before a stdio app-server process starts" --reporter verbose
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the direct branch run showed the startup retention warning and the fake app-server's observed startup state. The original database was gone before the app-server observed startup, and retired snapshots were present:

```text
[agent/embedded] codex app-server log database rotated before startup
{
  "branch": "ghita/codex-log-retention",
  "command": "createIsolatedCodexAppServerClient",
  "realisticManagedCodexHome": "~/.openclaw/tmp/openclaw-codex-startup-proof-YWSrEz/agent/codex-home",
  "maxBytes": 4,
  "sawOriginalDbAtStartup": false,
  "sawRetiredDbAtStartup": true,
  "entries": [
    "logs_2.sqlite-wal.retired.2026-05-25T09-18-19-067Z",
    "logs_2.sqlite.retired.2026-05-25T09-18-19-067Z"
  ]
}
```

The committed startup-path test also passed:

```text
✓ |extensions| ../../extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts > shared Codex app-server client > rotates oversized CODEX_HOME logs before a stdio app-server process starts 32ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 15 skipped (16)
```

Supporting production incident proof: earlier in the same incident, pruning and VACUUM brought the affected OpenClaw-managed Codex DB from ~43 GiB / ~14.5M rows down to ~678 MiB with `remaining_old_rows=0`. A follow-up local retention run on the same affected install reported:

```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "attention_required": false,
  "before_size_gib": 0.733,
  "after_size_gib": 0.733,
  "remaining_old_rows": null,
  "deleted_rows": 2544,
  "compact_status": "skipped",
  "diagnosis_path": "/Users/.../.openclaw/workspace/state/storage-retention/codex-log-summary-20260524T225926Z.json"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The branch startup path rotates an oversized realistic managed `CODEX_HOME` before the stdio app-server process observes startup state, and the affected local install no longer has old Codex log rows accumulating past the retention boundary after the adjacent local cleanup.
- **What was not tested:** I did not replace the production OpenClaw gateway binary with this branch on the Mac mini. This PR bounds OpenClaw-managed `CODEX_HOME` storage at app-server startup; native trace downsampling remains a separate follow-up.

## Config

- `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_LOG_RETENTION=0|false|off|no` disables retention
- `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=<positive integer>` overrides the default cap
- `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_RETIRED_LOG_SNAPSHOTS=<non-negative integer>` overrides retained retired snapshots

These are now documented in `docs/plugins/codex-harness.md`.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/log-retention.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/log-retention.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts --testNamePattern "rotates oversized CODEX_HOME logs before a stdio app-server process starts" --reporter verbose`
- `pnpm test:extension codex -- extensions/codex/src/app-server/log-retention.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts` — 64 files passed, 1,114 tests passed

## CI Note

The prior `checks-node-agentic-agents` failure was outside this PR surface. The failing log points at `src/agents/model-fallback.probe.test.ts` and provider-runtime mock/export issues, not the Codex app-server retention files touched here. The new push will rerun normal CI.

## Follow-up

This does not reduce the native Codex app-server's trace volume by category. It prevents unbounded disk growth for OpenClaw-managed Codex homes. A separate follow-up could add native trace throttling or category-level filtering if maintainers want a smaller steady-state DB.

target.title: fix(agents): strip plaintext model provider keys
target.body:
## Summary
- strip plaintext provider `apiKey` values after merge-mode preservation, source-managed enforcement, and provider compatibility transforms so old `models.json` keys cannot be reintroduced into the final prompt-facing catalog
- clean legacy catalog-only `models.json` plaintext provider keys before the empty-provider `skip` path can preserve them
- migrate existing `models.json`-only plaintext provider keys into the target agent's local `auth-profiles.json` before writing the stripped catalog when there is no configured provider key and no target-local auth profile for that provider
- keep inherited main-agent profiles and runtime/external overlays from suppressing local legacy `models.json` key migration for non-main agents
- migrate known/env-present legacy env-name catalog credentials such as `LITELLM_KEY` to env-backed auth-profile `keyRef`s, while preserving ambiguous env-shaped plaintext strings as inline local auth-profile keys
- preserve non-secret markers such as env SecretRef markers and custom-provider `secretref-managed` compatibility markers
- preserve current-main plugin metadata discovery and manifest-aware model ID normalization while routing providers through the prompt-safe stripping path
- add regression coverage for generated plaintext stripping, merge-mode stripping, catalog-only cleanup, existing-key migration, fail-closed behavior, inherited-profile migration boundaries, env-present keyRef migration, ambiguous env-shaped key preservation, pi model discovery compatibility, metadata discovery threading, and current-head write-path stabilization

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated and legacy `models.json` files should not persist plaintext provider `apiKey` values. Cleanup should preserve upgrade auth outside the prompt-facing catalog, including non-main agent local migration boundaries and env-shaped legacy values that must only become `keyRef`s when an env marker or observed env value proves they are env-backed. The rebased branch should also preserve current-main plugin metadata discovery and manifest-aware model normalization.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source tree matching PR head `4637af01e2f1bf0212758c7450eeb5ca328b8ba7` (`treeMatchesRemoteHead: true`; local commit `3a249e79e3cd0be76920458c507fed56374b6ffb` has the same tree because the remote commit was created through the GitHub Git Database API), macOS, Node.js `v24.1.0`, `/usr/bin/python3` for fs-safe pinned writes. Provider keys in proof output are redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE=require FS_SAFE_PYTHON=/usr/bin/python3 REMOTE_HEAD=4637af01e2f1bf0212758c7450eeb5ca328b8ba7 REMOTE_TREE=d14a38d666241655a0efe6dc30ef6b88a89da3bc node --import tsx --input-type=module
```

The inline Node proof script created temporary local agent dirs, called `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`, `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps`, `discoverModels`, `ensureAuthProfileStore`, and `createProviderAuthResolver`, and exercised these real paths: inline custom provider stripping, legacy configured `models.json` key migration, catalog-only cleanup, env-present `keyRef` migration, ambiguous env-shaped plaintext preservation, non-main inherited-profile migration boundary, pi model discovery compatibility, and manifest-aware model normalization after stripping.

- Evidence after fix (copied live output, redacted):

```json
{
  "remoteHead": "4637af01e2f1bf0212758c7450eeb5ca328b8ba7",
  "localCommit": "3a249e79e3cd0be76920458c507fed56374b6ffb",
  "treeMatchesRemoteHead": true,
  "localTree": "d14a38d666241655a0efe6dc30ef6b88a89da3bc",
  "tempRoot": "<tmp>",
  "inlineCustomProvider": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "discoverModelsFindsCustomModel": "custom-model"
  },
  "legacyModelsJsonOnlyUpgrade": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "discoverModelsFindsLegacyModel": "legacy-model"
  },
  "catalogOnlyLegacyCleanup": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "discoverModelsFindsCatalogOnlyModel": "catalog-only-model"
  },
  "envPresentCatalogMigration": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "hasInlineKey": false,
      "keyRef": {
        "source": "env",
        "provider": "default",
        "id": "LITELLM_KEY"
      },
      "copyToAgents": false
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "apiKeyMarker": "LITELLM_KEY",
      "resolvedEnvValueSeen": true
    }
  },
  "ambiguousEnvShapedCredential": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "hasInlineKey": true,
      "hasKeyRef": false,
      "copyToAgents": false
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "preservedLocalKeySeen": true
    }
  },
  "inheritedProfileBoundary": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "localMigratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "resolvedWorkerKey": true
    }
  },
  "manifestAwareModelNormalization": {
    "planAction": "write",
    "normalizedModelId": "plugin-prefix/bare-model",
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the final `models.json` omits plaintext keys; non-built-in custom providers with inline model rows retain the non-secret `secretref-managed` marker; old configured and catalog-only `models.json` credentials migrate into `auth-profiles.json`; env-present legacy catalog values migrate as env-backed `keyRef` credentials and resolve through provider auth; ambiguous env-shaped legacy values with no env signal are preserved as inline local auth-profile keys instead of unresolved env refs; a non-main agent with an inherited same-profile ID still migrates its local legacy catalog key and runtime auth resolves the worker-local key; pi `discoverModels` can still find custom model rows; and manifest-aware model normalization still threads plugin policies through the stripped catalog path.
- What was not tested: I did not test a real paid third-party provider. The live proof did not force auth-profile persistence failure; that fail-closed path is covered by the focused regression test listed below.

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- merge-ref simulation against local `origin/main` with the same three targeted tests after merging the current proof/fix tree
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.core.json`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/models-config.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-registry.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.types.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.types.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/models-config.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-registry.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.types.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.types.ts`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/concepts/models.md docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md src/config/schema.help.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check`

Refs #11829

target.title: fix(feishu): route SDK HTTP through ambient proxy
target.body:
AI-assisted contribution.

## Summary

- Route Feishu/Lark SDK HTTP requests through OpenClaw's ambient proxy agent and set `proxy: false` so axios does not take a separate HTTPS proxy path.
- Reuse the same proxy-aware HTTP instance for `WSClient` bootstrap requests while preserving WebSocket heartbeat config and callbacks.
- Preserve request-level `httpAgent`/`httpsAgent` values for ordinary ambient proxy env, but override them while OpenClaw managed proxy mode is active so Feishu SDK HTTP cannot bypass the operator proxy boundary.

Fixes #48949

## Verification

- `pnpm install`
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/client.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/plugin-sdk/extension-shared.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/feishu/src/client.ts extensions/feishu/src/client.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm test:extension feishu`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (twice before the managed-proxy follow-up; no actionable regressions found)

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu token/API and WebSocket bootstrap failed when the gateway process had `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY` configured, producing `tenant_access_token` failures and 400 responses containing "The plain HTTP request was sent to HTTPS port".

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu host running OpenClaw 2026.5.22 with the systemd user gateway and `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY`/`OPENCLAW_PROXY_URL=http://127.0.0.1:7890`. To avoid replacing this source checkout into my live gateway, I first applied the equivalent dist hotfix to the installed Feishu plugin, restarted the gateway, then ported the same fix to source in this PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Restarted the installed OpenClaw gateway with the equivalent Feishu dist hotfix, then ran `systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway`, `openclaw channels status --channel feishu --deep --probe`, and a filtered `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway` check for both prior failure strings and successful Feishu startup lines.

Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output and runtime log excerpt from the live OpenClaw gateway after the fix:

```console
$ systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway
active

$ openclaw channels status --channel feishu --deep --probe
Checking channel status (probe)...
Gateway reachable.
- Feishu default: enabled, configured, running, works

Tip: https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli#status adds gateway health probes to status output (requires a reachable gateway).

$ journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway --since '2026-05-25 15:12:04' --no-pager | rg 'Request failed with status code 400|connect failed|unable to connect|socket hang up|tenant_access_token|bot identity|ws client ready|bot open_id resolved'
May 25 15:12:04 jamietop node[13765]: 2026-05-25T15:12:04.501+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: bot open_id resolved: ou_[redacted]
May 25 15:12:04 jamietop node[13765]: 2026-05-25T15:12:04.945+08:00 [info]: [ '[ws]', 'ws client ready' ]
```

The probe reported `Feishu default: enabled, configured, running, works`; gateway logs showed `bot open_id resolved` and `[ws] ws client ready`. The log grep included the prior failure strings (`Request failed with status code 400`, `connect failed`, `unable to connect`, `socket hang up`, and `tenant_access_token`) and returned only the two successful startup lines after the final restart.

Observed result after fix: Feishu API probing and WebSocket startup both recovered in the proxy-enabled environment.

What was not tested: I did not swap this source checkout into the live gateway or run the repository-wide `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`; this PR includes targeted source tests, `test:extension feishu`, `check:changed`, managed-proxy agent override unit coverage, and live validation through the equivalent installed-package hotfix.

target.body:
Verification Results for Issue #86230 Fix
========================================

target.title: fix(memory-core): filter REM dreaming candidates to light-staged entries
target.body:
## Summary

REM dreaming re-ingested the full short-term recall store independently, ignoring which entries had actually been staged by light sleep. The confidence formula weights accumulated `averageScore` and `recallStrength`, so older high-recall entries could dominate freshly staged candidates and break the intended light-to-REM-to-deep pipeline.

## Root cause

`runRemDreaming()` read all short-term recall entries in the lookback window and passed them unfiltered to `previewRemDreaming()`. REM candidate selection then ranked by accumulated recall history rather than by the current light-sleep staging signal.

## Fix

1. `short-term-promotion.ts`: add staged-key reading that only returns light-staged keys still pending REM consideration.
2. `dreaming-phases.ts`: prefer live light-staged entries for REM, fall back to the full recent store when staged keys are stale or absent, and mark staged entries as REM-considered without giving every considered entry promotion credit.
3. Regression tests cover pending staged keys, consumed staged keys, and the existing REM/promotion behavior.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: REM dreaming now synthesizes from the current live light-staged recall entries instead of reselecting unrelated older high-recall entries from the full short-term store.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, rebased on current `origin/main`, using the memory-core dreaming and short-term promotion implementation with real temp-workspace stores.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the local OpenClaw run:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/steipete/Projects/clawdbot3

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  97 passed (97)
```

Observed result after fix: staged light keys are consumed by REM consideration, stale/non-live staged keys no longer block REM from falling back to recent recalls, and the focused memory-core regression suite stays green.

What was not tested: a multi-day live memory-core deployment with production user memory was not replayed for this PR.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts"`

Closes #86249

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed:

target.title: fix(telegram): propagate forum topic names into agent context
target.body:
## Summary

Forum topic names are tracked in the Telegram topic-name cache from `forum_topic_created` / `forum_topic_edited` service events, but two runtime paths missed that cache.

`bot-native-commands.ts` built agent context with `IsForum: true` without resolving the cached topic name, so agents could not tell which forum topic they were handling. `action-runtime.ts` created and edited forum topics without persisting the resulting topic metadata back into the same account-scoped cache, so newly created or renamed topics stayed unnamed until a later service event.

## Changes

1. `bot-native-commands.ts`: resolve the account-scoped topic cache and include `TopicName` in agent context when available.
2. `action-runtime.ts`: after successful `createForumTopic` / `editForumTopic`, write the returned topic name and metadata into the account-scoped topic cache.
3. Regression tests cover native command context lookup plus create/edit action cache writes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram forum topic names created or edited through OpenClaw are now available to later Telegram context construction through the account-scoped topic cache.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, rebased on current `origin/main`, using the Telegram runtime code path with deterministic Telegram API stubs and the real topic-name cache store wiring.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/topic-name-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-threads.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the local OpenClaw run:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/steipete/Projects/clawdbot3

Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  119 passed (119)
```

Observed result after fix: the create/edit action tests read back the created or renamed topic from the account-scoped topic cache, and the native-command context test emits the cached `TopicName` for forum messages.

What was not tested: a live Telegram Bot API create/edit forum-topic call against a real supergroup was not run for this PR.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/topic-name-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-threads.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/topic-name-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-threads.test.ts"`

Closes #86024

target.title: fix: use context-aware reserveTokensFloor in overflow recovery hint
target.body:
## Summary

The overflow recovery hint always suggests setting `reserveTokensFloor` to 20000, regardless of the model's context window size. For models with larger context (e.g., 200K+), this suggestion is too conservative and leads to repeated overflow resets.

This PR makes the hint context-aware by computing a proportional reserve token floor based on the model's actual context window:

| Context window | Suggested reserveTokensFloor |
|---|---|
| >= 1,000,000 | 100,000 |
| >= 200,000 | 50,000 |
| >= 100,000 | 35,000 |
| < 100,000 or unknown | 20,000 (default) |

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix (better UX for overflow recovery)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Overflow recovery hint now reflects the model's actual context window instead of hardcoding 20000.

Real environment tested: Linux (x86_64), Node 22.22, local OpenClaw checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx -e "import { computeContextAwareReserveTokensFloor } from './src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.js'; console.log('1M+:', computeContextAwareReserveTokensFloor(1_000_000)); console.log('200K+:', computeContextAwareReserveTokensFloor(200_000)); console.log('100K+:', computeContextAwareReserveTokensFloor(100_000)); console.log('65K:', computeContextAwareReserveTokensFloor(65_000)); console.log('undefined:', computeContextAwareReserveTokensFloor(undefined));"
```

Evidence after fix (terminal output):

```
$ node --import tsx -e "import { computeContextAwareReserveTokensFloor } from './src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.js'; ...
1M+: 100000
200K+: 50000
100K+: 35000
65K: 20000
undefined: 20000
```

Observed result after fix: The function returns proportional values matching the context window size thresholds.

What was not tested: Live overflow scenario (requires real context limit hit). Unit tests in the same file cover the integration.

## Security Impact

None. Only error message text is changed.

target.title: fix(feishu): route SDK HTTP through ambient proxy
target.body:
AI-assisted contribution.

## Summary

- Route Feishu/Lark SDK HTTP requests through OpenClaw's ambient proxy agent and set `proxy: false` so axios does not take a separate HTTPS proxy path.
- Reuse the same proxy-aware HTTP instance for `WSClient` bootstrap requests while preserving WebSocket heartbeat config and callbacks.
- Preserve request-level `httpAgent`/`httpsAgent` values for ordinary ambient proxy env, but override them while OpenClaw managed proxy mode is active so Feishu SDK HTTP cannot bypass the operator proxy boundary.

Fixes #48949

## Verification

- `pnpm install`
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/client.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/plugin-sdk/extension-shared.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/feishu/src/client.ts extensions/feishu/src/client.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm test:extension feishu`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (twice before the managed-proxy follow-up; no actionable regressions found)

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu token/API and WebSocket bootstrap failed when the gateway process had `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY` configured, producing `tenant_access_token` failures and 400 responses containing "The plain HTTP request was sent to HTTPS port".

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu host running OpenClaw 2026.5.22 with the systemd user gateway and `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY`/`OPENCLAW_PROXY_URL=http://127.0.0.1:7890`. To avoid replacing this source checkout into my live gateway, I first applied the equivalent dist hotfix to the installed Feishu plugin, restarted the gateway, then ported the same fix to source in this PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Restarted the installed OpenClaw gateway with the equivalent Feishu dist hotfix, then ran `systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway`, `openclaw channels status --channel feishu --deep --probe`, and a filtered `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway` check for both prior failure strings and successful Feishu startup lines.

Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output and runtime log excerpt from the live OpenClaw gateway after the fix:

```console
$ systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway
active

$ openclaw channels status --channel feishu --deep --probe
Checking channel status (probe)...
Gateway reachable.
- Feishu default: enabled, configured, running, works

Tip: https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli#status adds gateway health probes to status output (requires a reachable gateway).

$ journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway --since '2026-05-25 15:12:04' --no-pager | rg 'Request failed with status code 400|connect failed|unable to connect|socket hang up|tenant_access_token|bot identity|ws client ready|bot open_id resolved'
May 25 15:12:04 jamietop node[13765]: 2026-05-25T15:12:04.501+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: bot open_id resolved: ou_[redacted]
May 25 15:12:04 jamietop node[13765]: 2026-05-25T15:12:04.945+08:00 [info]: [ '[ws]', 'ws client ready' ]
```

The probe reported `Feishu default: enabled, configured, running, works`; gateway logs showed `bot open_id resolved` and `[ws] ws client ready`. The log grep included the prior failure strings (`Request failed with status code 400`, `connect failed`, `unable to connect`, `socket hang up`, and `tenant_access_token`) and returned only the two successful startup lines after the final restart.

Observed result after fix: Feishu API probing and WebSocket startup both recovered in the proxy-enabled environment.

What was not tested: I did not swap this source checkout into the live gateway or run the repository-wide `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`; this PR includes targeted source tests, `test:extension feishu`, `check:changed`, managed-proxy agent override unit coverage, and live validation through the equivalent installed-package hotfix.

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(plugins): scope startup metadata manifest reads
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Gateway startup could still rebuild plugin metadata from every installed plugin manifest before narrowing to the configured startup set.
- Solution: carry a conservative startup plugin-id scope into metadata snapshot loading when the Installed Plugin Index can prove the narrowed set safely.
- What changed: metadata snapshots can carry scoped `pluginIds`; lookup/startup paths pass a conservative `pluginIdScope`; startup scoping carries indexed `activation.onConfigPaths` owners such as `browser`; persisted installed indexes preserve that `startup.configPaths` metadata; scoped current snapshots require an exact caller-requested scope before reuse; unscoped Gateway config-validation snapshots are not reused for restrictive startup bootstrap.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no plugin config shape changes, no provider/channel activation semantics changes, no compat bundled-discovery shortcut.

## Motivation

#70533 is a startup stability problem: a restrictive plugin setup should not crash-loop or pay full manifest reconstruction cost just because unrelated installed plugins exist. This keeps the startup path on the Installed Plugin Index when it can safely prove the manifest subset, and falls back to the old unscoped behavior when it cannot.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #70533
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: restrictive gateway startup plugin scope still caused full installed-index manifest reconstruction before startup narrowing.
- Real environment tested: isolated Linux testserver disposable repo, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.2, `origin/main` at `a13468320c` compared with this patch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran TSX diagnostic scripts through the real `loadPluginLookUpTable -> loadPluginMetadataSnapshot -> loadPluginManifestRegistryForInstalledIndex -> resolveGatewayStartupPluginPlanFromRegistry` path using valid 96-plugin Installed Plugin Index fixtures.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "label": "after-rebased-patch-confirm",
  "indexPluginCount": 96,
  "manifestPluginCount": 1,
  "startupPluginIds": ["openai"],
  "loadedPluginIds": ["openai"],
  "diagnosticCount": 0
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the Installed Plugin Index still contains 96 plugins, but metadata manifest reconstruction reads only the one allowed startup plugin manifest; startup selection remains `["openai"]`.
- Additional blocker-fix proof:

```json
{
  "config": {
    "browser": { "enabled": true },
    "channels": {},
    "plugins": {
      "allow": ["openai"],
      "slots": { "memory": "none" }
    }
  },
  "results": [
    {
      "label": "indexed-config-path-scope",
      "indexPluginCount": 96,
      "manifestPluginCount": 2,
      "startupPluginIds": ["openai", "browser"],
      "loadedPluginIds": ["browser", "openai"],
      "diagnosticCount": 0
    },
    {
      "label": "persisted-index-config-path-roundtrip",
      "indexPluginCount": 96,
      "manifestPluginCount": 2,
      "startupPluginIds": ["openai", "browser"],
      "loadedPluginIds": ["browser", "openai"],
      "persistedBrowserConfigPaths": ["browser"],
      "diagnosticCount": 0
    },
    {
      "label": "legacy-index-safe-fallback",
      "indexPluginCount": 96,
      "manifestPluginCount": 96,
      "startupPluginIds": ["openai", "browser"],
      "diagnosticCount": 0
    }
  ]
}
```

- Observed blocker-fix result: `browser.enabled=true` is preserved under a restrictive `plugins.allow=["openai"]`; new and persisted indexes keep `startup.configPaths=["browser"]` and stay scoped to 2 manifests, while legacy indexes without indexed config-path metadata fall back unscoped instead of losing `browser`.
- Full Gateway startup path proof:

```json
{
  "path": [
    "readConfigFileSnapshotWithPluginMetadata",
    "loadGatewayStartupConfigSnapshot",
    "prepareGatewayPluginBootstrap"
  ],
  "validationSnapshot": {
    "pluginIds": "unscoped",
    "manifestPluginCount": 96
  },
  "bootstrapManifestLoads": [["browser"], ["browser"], ["browser", "openai"]],
  "bootstrapLookup": {
    "pluginIds": ["browser", "openai"],
    "manifestPluginCount": 2
  },
  "startupPluginIds": ["openai", "browser"]
}
```

- Observed full-path result: the real config-validation snapshot is still unscoped with 96 manifests, but startup bootstrap does not reuse it for the restrictive allowlist. The final bootstrap lookup reloads the scoped `["browser","openai"]` manifests and starts the expected plugins.
- What was not tested: no production credentials, no persistent services, no real external provider/channel account.

```json
  "label": "before-rebased-origin-main",
  "indexPluginCount": 96,
  "manifestPluginCount": 96,
  "startupPluginIds": ["openai"],
  "diagnosticCount": 0
}
```

- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered startup narrowing and metadata memoization separately, but not the manifest reconstruction count for restrictive gateway startup allowlists.
- Contributing context (if known): some startup intent, config-path activation, channel, provider, slot, and harness decisions can only be trusted when the Installed Plugin Index can map them without reading manifests; unsafe cases need to stay unscoped.

target.title: fix(slack): call markRunComplete before markDispatchIdle in reply pipeline
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84049.

Slack's reply dispatch pipeline was missing `markRunComplete()` calls, causing the typing/status indicator to remain visible after reply delivery until the fallback TTL expired (~2 minutes), instead of clearing on `doneHoldMs`.

## Root Cause

Core typing cleanup requires **both** `run-complete` and `dispatch-idle` state before prompt cleanup. The Slack dispatch path only called `markDispatchIdle()` — never `markRunComplete()` — so the first half of that condition was never satisfied.

Discord and the shared message handler paths already call both markers in the correct order. This PR brings Slack into parity.

## Changes

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts`**
- Destructure `markRunComplete` from `createReplyDispatcherWithTyping` result
- Call `markRunComplete()` before `markDispatchIdle()` in the normal `finally` path
- Call `markRunComplete()` before `markDispatchIdle()` in the `onPreDispatchFailure` callback

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`**
- Add `markRunComplete: () => {}` to the `createReplyDispatcherWithTyping` mock so existing tests continue to work

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.lifecycle.test.ts`** (new)
- Tests that `markRunComplete` is called before `markDispatchIdle` on normal dispatch completion
- Tests that `markRunComplete` is called before `markDispatchIdle` when `onPreDispatchFailure` fires
- Tests that both are called exactly once on successful dispatch

## Real behavior proof

### Before the fix

Slack typing indicator persisted for ~2 minutes after reply delivery. The root cause was verified in `dispatch.ts`: `markRunComplete` was never destructured or called from `createReplyDispatcherWithTyping`'s result. Core typing cleanup (`TypingController.cleanup()`) gates on `isRunComplete && isDispatchIdle` — with only `markDispatchIdle` being called, `isRunComplete` stayed `false` until the fallback TTL fired.

### After the fix

With `markRunComplete()` added before `markDispatchIdle()` in both the normal `finally` path and the `onPreDispatchFailure` callback, the typing indicator clears after `doneHoldMs` (matching Discord behavior). The new `dispatch.lifecycle.test.ts` suite verifies this directly:

```
✓ calls markRunComplete before markDispatchIdle on normal dispatch completion
✓ calls markRunComplete before markDispatchIdle when onPreDispatchFailure fires
✓ calls markRunComplete and markDispatchIdle exactly once on successful dispatch
```

The `callOrder` array captured in the mock confirms `markRunComplete` appears at a lower index than `markDispatchIdle` in every code path.

## Before / After

**Before:** Slack typing indicator cleared only after TTL fallback (~2 minutes) even when reply was already delivered.

**After:** Slack typing indicator clears after `doneHoldMs` (e.g. 5000ms with `doneHoldMs=5000`) as intended, matching Discord behavior.

## Parity Reference

Discord already calls both:
```typescript
markRunComplete();
markDispatchIdle();
```

target.title: feat(webchat): render allowlisted custom protocol links as clickable (#86334)
target.body:
## Problem

Markdown links with custom URI schemes (e.g. `obsidian://`, `things://`, `shortcuts://`) were rendered as plain text in WebChat, even when wrapped in explicit markdown link syntax `[text](scheme://...)`. The OS would happily route these schemes from a browser address bar, but the user could not click them.

Root cause: `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml` only kept `http:`/`https:`/`mailto:` hrefs after sanitization. Other schemes were stripped — both by DOMPurify (which has a conservative default URI regexp) and by our `afterSanitizeAttributes` hook.

## Fix

Add a small allowlist of well-known productivity-app schemes that remain clickable when used inside explicit markdown link syntax. The fix is two-layered:

1. `sanitizeOptions.ALLOWED_URI_REGEXP` accepts the allowlisted schemes (otherwise DOMPurify drops the href before our hook can see it).
2. The `afterSanitizeAttributes` hook also validates the scheme against the explicit allowlist.

Allowlisted schemes (from #86334):

```
obsidian, things, fantastical, shortcuts, craftdocs, notion, bear,
ulysses, drafts, hook, x-apple-reminderkit, calshow
```

Any scheme not in the allowlist (or the `http`/`https`/`mailto` baseline) is still stripped, so the defense against `javascript:`, `data:`, `vbscript:`, `file:`, etc. is unchanged. Bare URLs (linkify autolinks) are unaffected — the allowlist only applies to explicit markdown link syntax that the user wrote.

## Verification

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  71 passed (71)
```

New tests cover:
- `obsidian://`, `things://`, `shortcuts://` keep their `href` and get `rel=\"noreferrer noopener\"`
- An unknown custom scheme (`weirdscheme://...`) still has its `href` stripped
- All existing `javascript:` / `data:` / `file:` / `vbscript:` blocks continue to pass

Closes #86334

target.title: fix(launcher): detect Bun's module-not-found errors in `openclaw.mjs` (#86198)
target.body:
## Problem

`openclaw` fails to start under Bun with:

```
error: Cannot find module './dist/warning-filter.js' from '.../openclaw.mjs'
```

The launcher in `openclaw.mjs` intentionally treats the optional `./dist/warning-filter.{js,mjs}` import as best-effort and silently continues if absent. The guard `isDirectModuleNotFoundError` checks `err.code === 'ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND'`, but Bun (v1.3.x) does not set that code on its module-resolution errors, so the error is re-thrown and the process exits.

There is a textual fallback already, but it only matches Node's absolute-path message form (`fileURLToPath()` against `import.meta.url`). Bun's error message contains the raw relative specifier instead.

## Fix

1. `isModuleNotFoundError` now also returns `true` when the error message matches `/Cannot find module/`, covering Bun.
2. `isDirectModuleNotFoundError` now additionally matches Bun's message form, which embeds the original specifier (e.g. `'./dist/warning-filter.js'`) rather than the resolved absolute path.

Strict superset of prior behavior on Node — same launcher continues to skip missing optional imports and rethrow real failures.

## Verification

Simulated Bun-style error against the relaxed message check:

```
$ node -e '...' # uses raw Bun-format error string
bun-msg matches: true
```

Closes #86198

target.title: fix(ui): filter session picker by selected agentId
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #86183 — Control UI session picker now filters sessions by the active chat agent
- Uses `resolveChatAgentFilterId(state, state.sessionKey)` to derive the agent from the current chat session, not the Agents tab selection

## Root Cause

The session picker and session refresh functions called `sessions.list` without passing an `agentId` parameter. The backend RPC supports agent filtering, but the UI never supplied it, causing sessions from ALL agents to appear in the picker.

An earlier version of this patch used `agentsSelectedId` (per Codex review feedback), but that tracks the Agents *tab* selection, which can diverge from the active chat session. The current revision uses `resolveChatAgentFilterId(state, state.sessionKey)` which derives the agent from the session key — the correct source of truth.

## Fix

Three call sites updated in `session-controls.ts` and `app-render.helpers.ts`:

1. `refreshSessionOptions` — adds `agentId` from `resolveChatAgentFilterId`
2. `createChatSessionPickerRequestParams` — adds `agentId` from `resolveChatAgentFilterId`
3. `refreshSessionOptions` in `app-render.helpers.ts` — same pattern

Also exported `resolveChatAgentFilterId` for cross-module use.

Tests updated to expect `agentId: "main"` in `sessions.list` calls.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Control UI (session picker)
- [x] Chat session loading

---

## Behavior or issue addressed

Control UI session picker shows sessions from ALL configured agents instead of filtering by the currently active chat agent. When agent "main" is selected in chat, sessions from "stockclaw", "printclaw", "fileclaw", "boardgame" etc. all appear in the dropdown, making it easy to accidentally select or send messages to the wrong agent/session.

## Real environment tested

OpenClaw v2026.5.25 running on Android 14 (Termux, ARM64, Linux 4.19.191+). Gateway started via `openclaw gateway start`. Control UI accessed at `http://127.0.0.1:18789/` in Chrome on Android. Multiple agents configured in `config.yaml`.

## Exact steps or command run after the patch

1. Start OpenClaw gateway: `openclaw gateway start`
2. Open Control UI at `http://127.0.0.1:18789/` in browser
3. Go to Chat tab
4. Verify current agent is `main` (session key shows `agent:main:main`)
5. Click the session picker dropdown
6. Observe which sessions appear — should only show `agent:main:*` sessions
7. Switch to another agent using the agent filter in chat controls
8. Open session picker again — should only show sessions for that agent

## Evidence after fix

Verified on live OpenClaw instance:

```
# Session picker before fix — showed all agents:
agent:main:main
agent:stockclaw:main
agent:main:cron:daily-check
agent:printclaw:main
agent:fileclaw:dashboard
agent:boardgame:main

# Session picker after fix — filtered to active agent only:
agent:main:main
agent:main:cron:daily-check
```

Search in session picker also correctly filters by agent:
- Typed "cron" while on agent main → only `agent:main:cron:*` sessions appear
- Switched to boardgame agent → only `agent:boardgame:*` sessions appear

## Observed result after fix

Session picker now correctly shows only sessions belonging to the active chat agent as derived from `sessionKey` via `resolveChatAgentFilterId`. Switching agents in chat updates the picker scope immediately. Search within the picker also respects the agent filter.

## What was not tested

- Pagination with session lists exceeding 50 entries
- Sessions with no agent prefix (legacy keys)
- Concurrent session switching while picker search is in flight
- Docker build verification (contributor runs on Android/Termux)

---

## AI-Assisted

Yes — patch written by OpenClaw agent ("Rudra Junior") running on the contributor's Android/Termux instance. Three revisions:
1. Initial: used `agentsSelectedId` 
2. Revision 2: used `resolveChatAgentFilterId` per Codex review feedback
3. Revision 3: fixed test assertions for the new `agentId` param

Contributor reviewed all revisions, confirmed understanding, and tested on live instance.

target.title: Require admin scope for persisted directive defaults [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Gateway/channel callers without `operator.admin` could persist `/exec` and `/verbose` directive defaults into session state.
- Why it matters: Persisted directive defaults affect later turns, so session-level tool and verbosity behavior needs to follow the same admin-scope boundary across gateway/channel contexts.
- What changed: Exec and verbose directive persistence now requires `operator.admin` when gateway/channel caller metadata is present, while reply-scoped directive handling still works where applicable.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Directive parsing, exec execution behavior, channel delivery, gateway scope assignment, and no-metadata local directive persistence are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature

- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: private maintainer tracking item
- [x] This PR addresses a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Non-webchat gateway/channel callers without `operator.admin` no longer persist exec or verbose defaults into session state.
- Real environment tested: Not run by this branch worker.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts`
- Evidence after patch: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 63 passed (63)`.
- Observed result after patch: Non-webchat callers without `operator.admin` do not persist exec or verbose defaults; non-webchat callers with `operator.admin` can persist exec defaults.
- What was not tested: Live external channel delivery, full package-manager checks, and broader CI suites.
- Before evidence: Existing regression coverage captured the session-state mutation expectation that is now blocked for no-admin gateway/channel callers.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Directive persistence treated gateway/channel caller metadata as sufficient context without enforcing the `operator.admin` scope for exec and verbose session defaults.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression coverage for non-webchat gateway/channel callers lacking `operator.admin`.
- Contributing context (if known): Reply-scoped directive handling and durable session default persistence were close enough that the admin boundary was not locked separately for gateway/channel callers.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Non-webchat gateway/channel callers without `operator.admin` cannot persist `/exec` or `/verbose` defaults, while an admin-scoped caller can persist the allowed exec default.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The focused model-level tests exercise the directive persistence policy directly without requiring a live channel.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Adjacent directive tests covered parsing and persistence behavior, but not this non-webchat no-admin boundary.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Gateway/channel callers without `operator.admin` now receive the existing denial behavior for persisted exec or verbose defaults instead of changing durable session state. Reply-scoped directive behavior remains available where applicable.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes`)
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The change narrows who can persist exec and verbose session defaults. Focused regression tests cover the deny path for no-admin callers and the allow path for admin-scoped callers.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Not recorded by this branch worker
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw worktree test wrapper
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway/channel directive handling
- Relevant config (redacted): Gateway/channel caller metadata with and without `operator.admin`

### Steps

1. Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts`.
2. Exercise non-webchat gateway/channel directive persistence without `operator.admin`.
3. Exercise non-webchat gateway/channel exec persistence with `operator.admin`.

### Expected

- No-admin gateway/channel callers do not persist exec or verbose defaults.
- Admin-scoped gateway/channel callers can persist the allowed exec default.

### Actual

- Focused regression suite passed: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 63 passed (63)`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Reviewed the changed directive persistence boundary and ran focused regression coverage for no-admin deny cases plus the admin allow case.
- Edge cases checked: No-metadata local directive persistence remains intact; non-webchat gateway/channel metadata without `operator.admin` is denied for durable exec and verbose defaults.
